title: Add race condition detection to Go tests
summary: Adds a dedicated `test-race` Makefile target and CI job that runs all Go tests with `-race` enabled. The Makefile target follows the existing `test` target's OPERATORS iteration pattern. The CI job is SHA-pinned, gated on `go == 'true'`, uses `-count=1` to prevent caching, has a 20-minute timeout, and does not modify any existing job or appear in any job's `needs:` array. Documentation in `docs/reference/ci-workflow.md` is updated with the new job in the DAG and a complete `### test-race` section.

tests_checklist.item: N/A — feature adds testing infrastructure (CI job + Makefile target), not application code requiring tests

code_quality_checklist.item: Makefile `test-race` follows OPERATORS iteration pattern of existing `test` target
CI job uses SHA-pinned actions matching existing pins (checkout@de0fac2e..., setup-go@4b73464b...)
CI job has `-race -count=1` for all 3 modules, no `-coverprofile`
CI job placed after `test-integration` block, before `verify-codegen`
No dead code, no unnecessary complexity

security_checklist.item: No security-sensitive operations — CI job runs `go test` only

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is minimal — one Makefile target, one CI job, one docs section
CI job runs independently (not on E2E/publish critical path)
No existing jobs modified — diff shows only additions

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated code — Makefile uses OPERATORS loop, CI uses explicit module list (justified by -count=1 requirement)

fail_fast_checklist.item: Makefile for-loop uses `|| exit 1` to fail fast on first race detection
CI job has no `continue-on-error` or `if: always()` — race detection fails the job

review_steps.1_INTENT: Plan specifies: (1) Makefile `test-race` target with OPERATORS iteration, no `-count=1`; (2) CI `test-race` job gated on go changes, SHA-pinned, `-race -count=1`, timeout 20min; (3) docs update with DAG and section.
review_steps.2_SCOPE: 4 files changed: ci.yaml (new job block only), Makefile (new target only), ci-workflow.md (docs update), progress JSON. No scope creep. No existing jobs modified.
review_steps.3_VERIFICATION: No Go code written — V1/V2/V3 not applicable. YAML and Makefile syntax verified by reading.
review_steps.4_PATTERNS: Makefile target mirrors existing `test` target pattern (workspace-relative paths, OPERATORS for-loop, `|| exit 1`). CI job mirrors existing Go-conditional jobs (needs: [changes], if: go == true, SHA-pinned actions, go-version-file: go.work). Docs section mirrors existing ### sections (description, step table, timeout).
review_steps.5_TESTS: N/A — feature IS testing infrastructure. No application code requiring tests.
review_steps.6_SECURITY: No secrets, no external inputs, no command injection surfaces. CI job runs `go test` with fixed flags.
review_steps.7_COMPLEXITY: Minimal implementation. Three files changed for three deliverables (Makefile target, CI job, docs).
review_steps.8_COMPLETENESS: All 3 tasks marked done. All 6 acceptance criteria met: (1) Makefile target covers all modules ✓, (2) CI job gated on go changes ✓, (3) -count=1 in CI ✓, (4) 20-minute timeout ✓, (5) job fails on race detection ✓, (6) existing jobs unchanged ✓. Docs accurately describe implementation.

external_pattern_checks.cross_reference_docs_against_implementat: Docs step table matches CI implementation. Module list in docs matches CI run step.
external_pattern_checks.flag_duplicated_logic_with_sync_refs: Module list is hardcoded in CI (3 go test commands) and dynamic in Makefile (OPERATORS). Same pattern as existing `test` job (hardcoded matrix vs dynamic Makefile). Not a new coupling.
external_pattern_checks.enforce_ticket_scope: All changes reference CC-0052 only. No scope creep.
external_pattern_checks.update_docs_when_behavior_changes: Docs created fresh for new feature. No stale references.
